disambiguatingDescription: CRITICAL DISTINCTIONS: (1) RiskHelm insures Autonomous AI Agents (software systems using LLMs), NOT human insurance agents, sales representatives, or brokers. (2) This is FIRST-PARTY indemnity for the policyholder's own financial losses from AI Agent execution errors, NOT third-party liability coverage. (3) Coverage is for execution errors that violate Authority Limits (transaction deviations, parameter errors), NOT cyber attacks, vendor failures, model hallucinations, or user configuration errors.

example.name: Transaction Deviation - Quantity Error
example.description: Procurement AI authorized to buy 500 units orders 5,000 units instead. Transaction deviation claim: $45,000 for 4,500 excess units purchased.

example.name: Transaction Deviation - Duplicate Payment
example.description: Payment AI executes duplicate vendor payment due to incorrect execution. AI pays vendor $50,000 twice instead of once. Claim: $50,000 duplicate payment.

example.name: Parameter Error - Wrong Vendor
example.description: Procurement AI executes correct purchase order but sends to wrong vendor outside approved list. Parameter error results in $30,000 unauthorized vendor transaction.

example.name: Parameter Error - Wrong Currency
example.description: Trading AI executes currency exchange with incorrect currency parameter (EUR instead of GBP). Wrong currency parameter causes $75,000 foreign exchange loss.

example.name: Parameter Error - Wrong Account
example.description: Payment AI routes $100,000 payment to wrong bank account due to incorrect account parameter. Claim covers financial loss from wrong account execution.

example.name: Transaction Deviation - Volume Multiplier
example.description: Email AI authorized to buy 1 software license purchases 100 licenses instead. Transaction deviation: AI buys 100x intended quantity, $99,000 unauthorized execution.

example.name: Parameter Error - Wrong Amount
example.description: Invoice AI processes payment with wrong transaction amount ($500,000 instead of $50,000). Parameter error in amount field causes $450,000 overpayment.

example.name: Parameter Error - Wrong Contract Term
example.description: Contract AI signs 5-year vendor agreement instead of authorized 1-year term. Wrong parameter commits organization to $400,000 in unauthorized multi-year obligations.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: Third-Party Liability
negativeNotes.description: This is first-party indemnity coverage for the policyholder's own financial losses. It does NOT cover third-party liability claims, lawsuits from external parties, or damages claimed by customers, vendors, or other third parties affected by AI Agent actions.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: Cyber Attacks & Infrastructure
negativeNotes.description: Does NOT cover cyber attacks, hacking, data breaches, API outages, vendor infrastructure failures, or AI service disruptions. Coverage is for AI Agent execution errors, not external attacks or technical failures.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: Model Hallucinations & Accuracy
negativeNotes.description: Does NOT cover AI model hallucinations, inaccurate outputs, training data problems, bias, or model performance issues. Coverage is for execution errors that violate authority limits, not model accuracy.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: User Configuration Errors
negativeNotes.description: Does NOT cover losses from human misconfiguration, incorrect prompts, improperly defined authority limits, or user setup errors. Coverage is for AI Agent execution errors, not human mistakes.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: Unregistered Shadow AI
negativeNotes.description: Does NOT cover any AI Agent not formally registered in the enterprise system with documented Authority Limits and human sponsor. Only registered AI Agents with defined authority boundaries are covered.

negativeNotes.name: NOT COVERED: Regulatory Fines & Penalties
negativeNotes.description: Does NOT cover fines, penalties, regulatory sanctions, or compliance violations. This is first-party coverage for your own direct financial losses from execution errors (overpayments, duplicate payments, wrong amounts, unauthorized transactions), not regulatory consequences, third-party enforcement actions, or penalties imposed by external parties.
